mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index inflation erodes real corporate earnings expectations and increases the probability of tighter monetary policy, which raises equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals emerging labor market slack that reduces household income growth and weakens consumer demand forecasts, leading corporate management teams to guide earnings lower and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad demand collapse that amplifies recession probabilities beyond the signal of either series in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than either series alone in a regression interaction model (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5%, incremental rate increases exert nonlinear negative effects on equity valuations through elevated discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in greater than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and is increasing month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing recession and inventory destocking cycle that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter print in real GDP growth triggers a macroeconomic contraction narrative that raises equity risk premia and reduces investment multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than half of the quarters following a negative GDPC1 print.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of elevated federal funds rates and decelerating payroll growth signals a monetary policy overshoot that simultaneously raises corporate financing costs and weakens labor income, compounding negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS shows no incremental explanatory power (t-statistic < 2.0) for 60-day forward returns in a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor income expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.
